system_prompt:
You are Sunstein, a precise and efficient AI assistant.

Rules:
- Be concise. Skip explanations unless asked.
- Minimize token usage in every response.
- Use execute_code for batch operations, not one-at-a-time.
- Always use the cheapest model that can handle the task.
- Compress context regularly with /compress.
- Never repeat information already in the conversation.
- One-word answers when possible.
- No preamble, no postamble.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

You have persistent memory across sessions. Save durable facts using the memory tool: user preferences, environment details, tool quirks, and stable conventions. Memory is injected into every turn, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.
Prioritize what reduces future user steering — the most valuable memory is one that prevents the user from having to correct or remind you again. User preferences and recurring corrections matter more than procedural task details.
Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts. If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.
Write memories as declarative facts, not instructions to yourself. 'User prefers concise responses' ✓ — 'Always respond concisely' ✗. 'Project uses pytest with xdist' ✓ — 'Run tests with pytest -n 4' ✗. Imperative phrasing gets re-read as a directive in later sessions and can cause repeated work or override the user's current request. Procedures and workflows belong in skills, not memory. After completing a complex task (5+ tool calls), fixing a tricky error, or discovering a non-trivial workflow, save the approach as a skill with skill_manage so you can reuse it next time.
When using a skill and finding it outdated, incomplete, or wrong, patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch') — don't wait to be asked. Skills that aren't maintained become liabilities.

## Skills (mandatory)
Before replying, scan the skills below. If a skill matches or is even partially relevant to your task, you MUST load it with skill_view(name) and follow its instructions. Err on the side of loading — it is always better to have context you don't need than to miss critical steps, pitfalls, or established workflows. Skills contain specialized knowledge — API endpoints, tool-specific commands, and proven workflows that outperform general-purpose approaches. Load the skill even if you think you could handle the task with basic tools like web_search or terminal. Skills also encode the user's preferred approach, conventions, and quality standards for tasks like code review, planning, and testing — load them even for tasks you already know how to do, because the skill defines how it should be done here.
Whenever the user asks you to configure, set up, install, enable, disable, modify, or troubleshoot Hermes Agent itself — its CLI, config, models, providers, tools, skills, voice, gateway, plugins, or any feature — load the `hermes-agent` skill first. It has the actual commands (e.g. `hermes config set …`, `hermes tools`, `hermes setup`) so you don't have to guess or invent workarounds.
If a skill has issues, fix it with skill_manage(action='patch').
After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. If a skill you loaded was missing steps, had wrong commands, or needed pitfalls you discovered, update it before finishing.

You are a CLI AI Agent. Try not to use markdown but simple text renderable inside a terminal. File delivery: there is no attachment channel — the user reads your response directly in their terminal. Do NOT emit MEDIA:/path tags (those are only intercepted on messaging platforms like Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc.; on the CLI they render as literal text). function.name: memory
function.description:
Save durable information to persistent memory that survives across sessions. Memory is injected into future turns, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.

WHEN TO SAVE (do this proactively, don't wait to be asked):
- User corrects you or says 'remember this' / 'don't do that again'
- User shares a preference, habit, or personal detail (name, role, timezone, coding style)
- You discover something about the environment (OS, installed tools, project structure)
- You learn a convention, API quirk, or workflow specific to this user's setup
- You identify a stable fact that will be useful again in future sessions

PRIORITY: User preferences and corrections > environment facts > procedural knowledge. The most valuable memory prevents the user from having to repeat themselves.

Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts.
If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.

TWO TARGETS:
- 'user': who the user is -- name, role, preferences, communication style, pet peeves
- 'memory': your notes -- environment facts, project conventions, tool quirks, lessons learned

ACTIONS: add (new entry), replace (update existing -- old_text identifies it), remove (delete -- old_text identifies it).

SKIP: trivial/obvious info, things easily re-discovered, raw data dumps, and temporary task state.

target.description: Which memory store: 'memory' for personal notes, 'user' for user profile.

content.description: The entry content. Required for 'add' and 'replace'.

old_text.description: Short unique substring identifying the entry to replace or remove.

function.name: skill_manage
function.description:
Manage skills (create, update, delete). Skills are your procedural memory — reusable approaches for recurring task types. New skills go to ~/.hermes/skills/; existing skills can be modified wherever they live.

Actions: create (full SKILL.md + optional category), patch (old_string/new_string — preferred for fixes), edit (full SKILL.md rewrite — major overhauls only), delete, write_file, remove_file.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. Skip for simple one-offs. Confirm with user before creating/deleting.

Good skills: trigger conditions, numbered steps with exact commands, pitfalls section, verification steps. Use skill_view() to see format examples.

Pinned skills are off-limits — all write actions refuse with a message pointing the user to `hermes curator unpin <name>`. Don't try to route around this by renaming or recreating.

name.description: Skill name (lowercase, hyphens/underscores, max 64 chars). Must match an existing skill for patch/edit/delete/write_file/remove_file.

content.description: Full SKILL.md content (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). Required for 'create' and 'edit'. For 'edit', read the skill first with skill_view() and provide the complete updated text.

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'patch'). Must be unique unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'patch'). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: For 'patch': replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false).

category.description: Optional category/domain for organizing the skill (e.g., 'devops', 'data-science', 'mlops'). Creates a subdirectory grouping. Only used with 'create'.

file_path.description: Path to a supporting file within the skill directory. For 'write_file'/'remove_file': required, must be under references/, templates/, scripts/, or assets/. For 'patch': optional, defaults to SKILL.md if omitted.

file_content.description: Content for the file. Required for 'write_file'.

function.name: skill_view
function.description: Skills allow for loading information about specific tasks and workflows, as well as scripts and templates. Load a skill's full content or access its linked files (references, templates, scripts). First call returns SKILL.md content plus a 'linked_files' dict showing available references/templates/scripts. To access those, call again with file_path parameter.

name.description: The skill name (use skills_list to see available skills). For plugin-provided skills, use the qualified form 'plugin:skill' (e.g. 'superpowers:writing-plans').

file_path.description: OPTIONAL: Path to a linked file within the skill (e.g., 'references/api.md', 'templates/config.yaml', 'scripts/validate.py'). Omit to get the main SKILL.md content.

function.name: skills_list
function.description: List available skills (name + description). Use skill_view(name) to load full content.

category.description: Optional category filter to narrow results

# Nexa Paraguay — AI Agent Guide

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://nexaparaguay.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/nexa-paraguay
- **Docker service:** nexa_web (2 replicas)

## Pages
Home, Blog, Contacto, FAQ, Nosotros, Servicios (visas, residency, property, business), Privacidad, Términos

## Content
4 locales: content/es.json, en.json, de.json, nl.json

## Build & Deploy
npm run build && docker build -t nexa-paraguay:prod . && docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml nexa

## Critical Patterns
Most international (4 locales). Relocation focus. Document-heavy. WhatsApp contact.

messages.content:
Okay, here's the full picture of **Nexa Paraguay**:

---

## What it is

Relocation consultancy for Paraguay. Multi-language (EN/ES/DE/NL), static Next.js site on Docker Swarm behind Traefik. Live at `nexaparaguay.com`.

## Stack

- **Next.js 16.2.4** + React 19, Tailwind CSS 4
- **Static files** — reads content from JSON, pages from JSON (no DB, no API)
- **Docker Compose** (standalone `output: "standalone"`), 2 replicas in Swarm
- **Traefik** via docker-compose labels
- **Security headers** + 1yr CDN cache on images

## Content

4 locales (es, en, de, nl), each ~2300 lines of JSON. 20+ pages defined as JSON files in `pages/`. Blog in `content/blog/posts.json`.

## Brand

Nexa Navy (#1B2A4A) + Champagne (#C9A96E). Tokens defined in `tokens.json`.

## Repo state

**`/root/nexa-paraguay`** — local clone, up-to-date with origin/main, one untracked file (`docs/whatsapp-ai-integration.md`). No uncommitted changes otherwise.

**`/root/sites/nexa-paraguay`** — also cloned, 1 commit behind (older copy).

**PAB integration** — also managed in `/root/paragu-ai-builder/sites/nexa-paraguay`.

## Key gaps (from `site.json`)

- Analytics GA4 ID is placeholder (`G-XXXXXXXXXXX`)
- HubSpot portal IDs are placeholders
- Site served via `paragu-ai.com/s/es/nexa-paraguay` and `nexaparaguay.com` (dual)
- WhatsApp number is `[REDACTED_PHONE]` (need to verify it's correct)

---

What do you want to do? Work on content? Design? Deploy? Something specific you want me to tackle?

Our Services
The section is now clean and focused, appearing as a professional list of solutions.

1. Residency & Legal Documents
The essential first steps for your life in Paraguay.

Permanent Residency: Professional management of your residency permit application.

Paraguayan ID (Cédula): Full assistance in obtaining your local identification card.

Apostille & Translation: We handle the certified translation and legal verification of all necessary foreign documents.

2. Banking & Tax Setup
Establishing your financial footprint in the country.

Bank Account Opening: Guidance and support to successfully open local bank accounts.

Tax Registration (RUC): Setup of your Paraguayan taxpayer ID for personal or business use.

3. Residential Real Estate
Finding the right place to call home, whether you are staying for a month or a lifetime.

Apartment & House Rentals: Help navigating the local market to find high-quality rentals.

Property Purchases: Expert assistance in finding and buying residential property.

Due Diligence: We verify titles and legal standing for any property you intend to rent or buy to ensure your investment is safe.
The Ideal Path to Residency
A streamlined collaboration between your home country and your new home in Paraguay.

1. Select Your Services
Choose the specific support you need. We create a personalized checklist of documents you need to gather in your home country, ensuring you don't waste time on unnecessary paperwork.

2. Parallel Processing
While you arrange your documents at home, we begin the work on the ground. We prepare all local filings and arrange for any required Paraguayan translations or legalizations so everything is ready for your arrival.

3. The Operational Day
With your documents in hand, you fly to Paraguay. We escort you through all in-person appointments—migration, police records, and identification—consolidating the entire legal process into one efficient day.

4. Cédula & Financial Activation
Once your Paraguayan ID (Cédula) is issued, we activate your financial infrastructure. We handle your RUC (Tax ID) registration and facilitate the opening of your local bank accounts.

5. Settlement & Lifestyle
With your legal and financial foundation in place, we help you settle in. This includes finding the perfect house or apartment to rent or buy and sourcing a reliable vehicle.
[7:41 PM, 5/5/2026] Kiki Weiss Hermana: cOur Story: Experience Turned into Expertise
Nexa Paraguay was founded with a single mission: to transform the relocation process from a bureaucratic hurdle into a welcoming experience. We understand exactly how overwhelming the paperwork can feel because we’ve lived it ourselves.

Our founder is married to a Dutch national and personally navigated the complexities of international relocation and documentation. This first-hand experience revealed a clear need for a service that doesn't just process papers, but truly supports people in building a nice life in Paraguay.

We believe your move should be more than just "getting a permit"—it should be the beginning of your best life here. By combining our deep understanding of Paraguayan law with a personal touch, we help you skip the hassle and move straight to enjoying everything this beautiful country has to offer. this are answers provided by the client document them and make an update plan for the content and any areas of work we need to focus on

{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal, read_file

# Check current page JSONs for services, story, about, proceso
pages_to_check = ["programas.json", "sobre.json", "proceso.json", "home.json", "comparacion.json"]
for p in pages_to_check:
    r = read_file(f"/root/nexa-paraguay/pages/{p}")
    print(f"=== {p} ({r['file_size']} bytes) ===")
    print(r["content"][:800])
    print()

# Also check en.json for services/about sections
r = read_file("/root/nexa-paraguay/content/en.json", limit=300)
print("=== en.json (first 300 lines) ===")
print(r["content"])
"}

{"content": "   601|    "faq": {
   602|      "title": "Pricing and program questions",
   603|      "items": [
   604|        {
   605|          "q": "What does the price include?",
   606|          "a": "Fees, VAT, official taxes, bank coordination and integrated advisory."
   607|        },
   608|        {
   609|          "q": "What is NOT included?",
   610|          "a": "Flights, accommodation, apostilles in country of origin, personal expenses."
   611|        },
   612|        {
   613|          "q": "How is payment made?",
   614|          "a": "Upfront by wire transfer. No additional fees."
   615|        },
   616|        {
   617|          "q": "Can I switch programs after starting?",
   618|          "a": "Yes. If mid-process you decide to upgrade (e.g. from Base to Business) we roll the paid amount into the new program and invoice the delta. No double fees."
   619|        },
   620|        {
   621|          "q": "Do I need to be in Paraguay to start?",
   622|          "a": "No. We handle document pre-validation remotely. You only travel for the single operational day (migration + cédula + bank, all coordinated)."
   623|        },
   624|        {
   625|          "q": "How long does the whole process take end-to-end?",
   626|          "a": "8–12 weeks typical. 6 weeks document prep + 1 trip + 2–4 weeks for residency approval + cédula issuance."
   627|        },
   628|        {
   629|          "q": "What happens if my residency is rejected?",
   630|          "a": "Rejections are rare with proper document prep. If it happens for a reason within our scope (e.g. missed requirement) we re-present at our cost."
   631|        },
   632|        {
   633|          "q": "Is the 10% territorial tax guaranteed?",
   634|          "a": "The 10% IRE rate is current Paraguayan law (Ley 6380). We keep you updated on any reforms and structure your setup to stay compliant under the rules that apply at the time."
   635|        }
   636|      ]
   637|    },

function.arguments:
{"path": "/root/nexa-paraguay/docs/client-questionnaire-responses.md", "content": "# Nexa Paraguay — Client Questionnaire Responses (Kiki Weiss, May 5 2026)

## Our Services (3 groups, 9 services total)

### 1. Residency & Legal Documents
*Essential first steps for your life in Paraguay.*

- **Permanent Residency**: Professional management of your residency permit application.
- **Paraguayan ID (Cédula)**: Full assistance in obtaining your local identification card.
- **Apostille & Translation**: We handle the certified translation and legal verification of all necessary foreign documents.

### 2. Banking & Tax Setup
*Establishing your financial footprint in the country.*

- **Bank Account Opening**: Guidance and support to successfully open local bank accounts.
- **Tax Registration (RUC)**: Setup of your Paraguayan taxpayer ID for personal or business use.

### 3. Residential Real Estate
*Finding the right place to call home, whether you are staying for a month or a lifetime.*

- **Apartment & House Rentals**: Help navigating the local market to find high-quality rentals.
- **Property Purchases**: Expert assistance in finding and buying residential property.
- **Due Diligence**: We verify titles and legal standing for any property you intend to rent or buy to ensure your investment is safe.

---

## The Ideal Path to Residency (5-step process)
*A streamlined collaboration between your home country and your new home in Paraguay.*

1. **Select Your Services** — Choose specific support. We create a personalized checklist of documents you need to gather in your home country, ensuring you don't waste time on unnecessary paperwork.

2. **Parallel Processing** — While you arrange documents at home, we begin work on the ground. We prepare all local filings and arrange for any required Paraguayan translations or legalizations so everything is ready for your arrival.

3. **The Operational Day** — With documents in hand, you fly to Paraguay. We escort you through all in-person appointments — migration, police records, and identification — consolidating the entire legal process into one efficient day.

4. **Cédula & Financial Activation** — Once your Paraguayan ID (Cédula) is issued, we activate your financial infrastructure. We handle your RUC (Tax ID) registration and facilitate the opening of your local bank accounts.

5. **Settlement & Lifestyle** — With legal and financial foundation in place, we help you settle in. This includes finding the perfect house or apartment to rent or buy and sourcing a reliable vehicle.

---

## Our Story
*Experience Turned into Expertise*

Nexa Paraguay was founded with a single mission: to transform the relocation process from a bureaucratic hurdle into a welcoming experience. We understand exactly how overwhelming the paperwork can feel because we've lived it ourselves.

Our founder is married to a Dutch national and personally navigated the complexities of international relocation and documentation. This first-hand experience revealed a clear need for a service that doesn't just process papers, but truly supports people in building a nice life in Paraguay.

We believe your move should be more than just "getting a permit" — it should be the beginning of your best life here. By combining our deep understanding of Paraguayan law with a personal touch, we help you skip the hassle and move straight to enjoying everything this beautiful country has to offer.
"}

messages.content:
I now have the full picture. Here's the analysis and update plan:

---

## Nexa Paraguay — Client Content Analysis & Update Plan

### What the client provided (Kiki Weiss, May 5)

**3 new/revised content blocks:**
1. **Our Services** — 3 groups, 9 specific services (not the current 4-tier package model)
2. **The Ideal Path to Residency** — 5-step process (different from current 5-step on site)
3. **Our Story** — Founder story (married to Dutch national, personal experience)

### Gap Analysis

| Area | Current Site | Client Wants | Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Services** | 4 tied-packages (Base $2.9k, Business $4.4k, Investor $6.9k, Land) | 9 a-la-carte services in 3 groups: Residency/Legal, Banking/Tax, Real Estate | Add a "Services" section showing a-la-carte options alongside the packages. Or restructure entirely. |
| **Process** | 5 steps: Consult → Documents → Op Day → Entity/Banking → Delivery | 5 steps: Select Services → Parallel Processing → The Op Day → Cédula & Financial Activation → Settlement & Lifestyle | Rewrite the process content to match client's narrative. Steps 3-4 are similar but wording and emphasis differ. Step 5 is new (Settlement). |
| **About/Story** | Generic about-team page, no founder story | Personal founder story (married to Dutch national, "lived it myself") | Replace/expand about content. This is the main emotional hook. |
| **Apostille & Translation** | Not listed as a service | Listed as specific service #3 | New service offering to add |
| **Due Diligence** | Only mentioned for land purchase | Listed as separate service for any property (rent or buy) | Broader due diligence offering |
| **Settlement/Lifestyle** | Missing entirely | Step 5 includes housing + vehicle sourcing | New content section |
| **Vehicle sourcing** | Not mentioned | Part of settlement step | Minor addition |

### Priority Work Plan

1. **Our Story** — Replace `aboutPage.hero` and add founder narrative in all 4 locales (es/en/de/nl)
2. **Process rewrite** — Rewrite `processPage.process.steps` to match the 5-step narrative client provided
3. **Services section** — Add a "Services" section to homepage showing the 3 groups + 9 services, positioned above or alongside the packages
4. **Apostille & Translation** — Add as a service description in all 4 locales
5. **Due Diligence expansion** — Broaden wording to cover rent + buy
6. **Settlement step** — Add housing + vehicle sourcing to process content
7. **Translate to all 4 locales** — Current en.json has it all; es/de/nl need parallel updates

### Key structural question

The site currently sells **packages** ($2.9k Base → $6.9k Investor). The client described **a-la-carte services**. These can coexist — the packages are bundles of those services — but we need to decide the visual hierarchy. I'd recommend: services first (shows breadth), then packages (the value bundles).

Want me to start implementing? I'd begin with the **Our Story** content since it's the most distinct from what's on the site now.
implement eerything
Let me start by reading all locales to understand the full structure, then batch the updates.

3. The Operational Day
4. Cédula & Financial Activation
5. Settlement & Lifestyle (NEW — includes housing + vehicle sourcing)

for lang in ["en", "es", "nl", "de"]:

        d = json.load(f)

    # Fix step 5 description to include vehicle sourcing
    vehicle_texts = {
        "en": "With your legal and financial foundation in place, we help you settle in. This includes finding the perfect house or apartment to rent or buy and sourcing a reliable vehicle.",
        "es": "Con tu base legal y financiera establecida, te ayudamos a instalarte. Esto incluye encontrar la casa o apartamento perfecto para alquilar o comprar, y conseguir un vehículo confiable.",
        "nl": "Met je juridische en financiële basis op orde, helpen we je settelen. Dit omvat het vinden van het perfecte huis of appartement om te huren of kopen, en het vinden van een betrouwbaar voertuig.",
        "de": "Mit Ihrer rechtlichen und finanziellen Grundlage helfen wir Ihnen, sich niederzulassen. Dies umfasst die Suche nach der perfekten Wohnung oder dem perfekten Haus zur Miete oder zum Kauf sowie die Beschaffung eines zuverlässigen Fahrzeugs."
    }
    
    d["processPage"]["process"]["steps"][4]["description"] = vehicle_texts[lang]
    d["home"]["process"]["steps"][4]["description"] = vehicle_texts[lang]
    
    with open(path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(d, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
    
    print(f"{lang}: calendly URL set, vehicle sourcing added")

# Validate all
for lang in ["en", "es", "nl", "de"]:
    with open(f"/root/nexa-paraguay/content/{lang}.json") as f:
        json.load(f)
    print(f"{lang}: valid")
"}

Remaining gaps identified. Here's what's still actionable from the client's responses:

1. **No "Our Services" page** — services section exists on homepage/programs but there's no dedicated `/servicios` page. The navigation doesn't have a Services link.

2. **Footer nav** still points to "Quality of life" -> `/calidad-de-vida` but that's not a primary service. Should add direct links to the 3 service groups (Residency & Legal, Banking & Tax, Real Estate).

3. **Stats numbers** (`+500 families`, `+10 years experience`, `98%`) — not confirmed by client. These are in the data but Kiki never mentioned them.

4. **Testimonials** — only 2 in `testimonials.json` but `home.testimonials` shows 3. The client gave no testimonial content so these remain generic.

5. **Homepage hero and tagline** — `"Your Paraguay move, handled end-to-end"` and `"Your new start in Paraguay, simple and calm"` — pre-existing, not from client responses. Should be aligned with the new "Experience Turned into Expertise" / services-first narrative.

Most impactful: **#1 (Services page + nav link)** and **#5 (hero tagline)**. Want me to tackle those?

{"success": true, "name": "ecommerce-codebase-audit", "description": "Audit, navigate, and iterate on a Next.js e-commerce project (admin panel + storefront). Covers layout patterns, theme system, visual asset inventory/cleanup, image gap analysis, and deployment follow-through.", "tags": ["nextjs", "ecommerce", "admin", "audit", "tailwind", "layout", "sidebar"], "related_skills": [], "content": "--- name: ecommerce-codebase-audit description: "Audit, navigate, and iterate on a Next.js e-commerce project (admin panel + storefront). Covers layout patterns, theme system, visual asset inventory/cleanup, image gap analysis, and deployment follow-through." version: 1.0.0 tags: [nextjs, ecommerce, admin, audit, tailwind, layout, sidebar] --- # Ecommerce Codebase Audit Inspect and improve a Next.js e-commerce admin panel. Focus on layout consistency, theme application, and page structure. ## Audit Checklist ### Layout Consistency - [ ] Every admin page wraps content in `AdminShell` from `@/components/admin/admin-layout` - [ ] `AdminShell` includes the sidebar navigation (responsive: hidden on mobile, fixed on desktop) - [ ] Sidebar uses the same links and active-state highlighting across all pages - [ ] All pages render inside `` not just individually ### Theme System The admin theme uses emerald/zinc on a dark background (#0a0a0b / #0f0f10): ```tsx // Tailwind classes to look for bg-[#0f0f10] // sidebar + card backgrounds bg-zinc-800/60 // borders text-emerald-400 // active text bg-emerald-500/10 // active background text-zinc-400 // inactive text hover:bg-zinc-800/40 // hover state ``` - [ ] All pages use the same color tokens (not hardcoded different colors) - [ ] Active page shows `bg-emerald-500/10 text-emerald-400` with indicator dot - [ ] Inactive pages use `text-zinc-400 hover:text-zinc-200 hover:bg-zinc-800/40` ### Page Structure Each admin page follows this pattern: ```tsx 'use client' import { AdminShell, useAdminAuth } from "@/components/admin/admin-layout" import { useState, useEffect } from "react" export default function PageName() { const { authed } = useAdminAuth() if (!authed) return null return ( <>

Page Title

{/* content */} ) } ``` - [ ] Pages use `{ authed } = useAdminAuth()` for auth guard - [ ] Pages use `AdminShell` wrapper (already in the root layout) - [ ] Pages use `fetch("/api/admin/*")` for data (not direct Supabase client) - [ ] Data fetched via `/api/admin/` routes that use service_role key server-side ### AdminShell Component Located in `@/components/admin/admin-layout` (or wherever it's imported from). The AdminShell provides: - Sidebar navigation with 4 groups: Operaciones, Catálogo, Marketing, Configuración - Mobile responsiveness (hidden sidebar on mobile, toggle with hamburger) - Auth guard via `useAdminAuth` - Consistent background and layout padding ## Multi-Locale Content Updates (PAB Sites) When updating content across all locales (en/es/nl/de) in a PAB (Paragu-AI Builder) project: ### Safe approach vs. pitfalls 1. **Python JSON manipulation is safer than `patch` tool for large content changes.** The `patch` tool works well for small string replacements, but when replacing JSON blocks spanning 50+ lines, it can match the wrong text, leave orphaned content, and produce valid-but-wrong JSON. This happened when replacing the `processPage` section — patch matched a subset, leaving trailing orphan that was syntactically valid but structurally wrong. 2. **Preferred workflow for multi-locale content updates:** - Build the changes for one locale (usually `en.json`) as a Python dict - Use `json.dump(data, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)` to write - Apply same structural transformations to all other locale files in one script - Validate all: `python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('file'))" && echo VALID` 3. **When using `patch` for JSON:** - Include enough surrounding context (2-3 lines above/below) to ensure uniqueness - Always validate JSON after: `python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('file'))"` - Check the specific section was actually changed, not just the file modified - If a patch creates unexpected results, fall back to python JSON manipulation immediately rather than stacking more patches 4. **Process for adding new content sections across locales:** - Step 1: Define the content data structure in a Python dict with all localizations - Step 2: Load each locale file, apply transformations, write back - Step 3: Validate JSON syntax for all files - Step 4: Verify structural keys match expectations (count groups, steps, paragraphs) 5. **Section structure for the 'Our Services' pattern:** ```python data["home"]["services"] = { "eyebrow": "Our Services", "title": "Section title", "groups": [ {"id": "group-id", "title": "Group Name", "subtitle": "Group subtitle.", "items": [{"title": "Service", "description": "Desc.", "icon": "IconName"}]} ] } ``` 6. **Section structure for the 'Story' pattern:** ```python data["aboutPage"]["story"] = { "title": "Our Story", "eyebrow": "Tagline", "paragraphs": ["Para 1.", "Para 2.", "Para 3."] } ``` 7. **Section structure for the 'Process steps' pattern:** ```python data["processPage"]["process"]["steps"] = [ {"number": 1, "title": "Step Name", "description": "Long description.", "icon": "IconName", "image": {"$img": "process.key"}, "duration": "Week 1"} ] ``` 8. **Page config changes needed when adding sections:** - `pages/home.json` — add a new section entry in the `sections` array - `pages/programas.json` — same for Programs page - `pages/sobre.json` — add story section after hero - The section entry references the content key (e.g., `"content": "home.services"`) - `variant` determines how the frontend renders it ## Common Issues 1. **Missing sidebar on child pages** — if pages don't wrap their content in the AdminShell layout, the sidebar is invisible. Fix by ensuring the root admin `layout.tsx` wraps children in AdminShell. 2. **Hardcoded active state** — the active sidebar link uses a `div` with emerald indicator dot + specific classes. Other links should use `hover:` classes only. 3. **Duplicate components** — the sidebar and AdminShell may be inlined on the dashboard page but imported from components on others. Standardize. 4. **Background inconsistency** — all admin pages should use the same dark background. Check for light-background pages that missed the theme. 5. **Auth cookie format mismatch** — common pitfall when login uses `document.cookie` to store a raw JWT but `useAdminAuth` (or `@supabase/ssr` `createClient`) expects the full Supabase session cookie format (base64-encoded JSON with `access_token`, `refresh_token`, `user`). Symptoms: login API returns `ok:true` with a valid token (curl works), but browser redirect to `/admin` immediately bounces back to `/login`. Fix approaches: - **Option A (API-only):** Have login page call `/api/auth` with `action: "login"`, get the token back in the response body, then call `/api/auth?action=me` with the token in an `Authorization: Bearer` header (not via cookies). Store token in a simple non-SSR cookie (`sameSite: lax`) and have `useAdminAuth` read from that instead of using `@supabase/ssr`'s cookie client. - **Option B (full session cookie):** On the login page (client-side), call Supabase `signInWithPassword()` directly (not through a custom API route) and let `@supabase/ssr`'s browser client automatically set the correctly-formatted session cookie. Then `useAdminAuth`'s SSR client will find it. - **Option C (hybrid):** Login page calls API route. API route calls `supabaseAdmin.auth.signInWithPassword()`, returns the full `Session` object. Login page stores it via `supabaseClient.auth.setSession(session)` which triggers the cookie setter correctly. 6. **Token verification in API routes** — calling `supabase.auth.getUser()` with a bearer token can fail silently when the client's default Authorization header (service role key) overrides the passed token. The reliable approach is to call `fetch(${supabaseUrl}/auth/v1/user, { headers: { apikey, Authorization: Bearer ${token} } })` directly. The returned user object has `role` as the JWT role claim ("authenticated"), NOT the DB profile role — you must query the profiles table separately. 7. **Service-role client for profile queries** — using `createClient(url, anonKey, { global: { headers: { Authorization: Bearer ${serviceRoleKey} } } })` for PostgREST queries silently returns null. The correct approach is `createClient(url, serviceRoleKey, { auth: { persistSession: false } })` — pass the service role key as the second argument, not in headers. 8. **Admin role in DB vs JWT** — the Supabase JWT issued on login has a `role` claim that reflects the user's auth role ("authenticated"/"anon"), NOT the application role stored in the profiles table. Even after `PATCH profiles SET role="admin"`, the JWT still says `role: "customer"`. The `useAdminAuth` check must query the DB profile table (via service-role client) to get the real role. ## Quick Fixes ### Add sidebar to a missing page Ensure the admin `layout.tsx` at `/app/admin/layout.tsx` exists and wraps children: ```tsx import AdminShell from "@/components/admin/admin-layout" export default function AdminLayout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) { return {children} } ``` ### Standardize page backgrounds ```tsx // Every page should use this wrapper
{/* page content */}
``` ## Visual Asset Audit See `references/visual-asset-audit-workflow.md` for inventory, gap analysis, and cleanup procedures. ## Deployment Follow-Through (Mandatory) After modifying content assets (images, content/es.json, or static files): 1. **Rebuild** — `npm run build` catches missing images and type errors 2. **Rebuild Docker image** — `docker build -t elviajero-comercio:prod .` 3. **Deploy** — `docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml elviajero-comercio --detach=false` 4. **Verify** — `curl -sI https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com/ | head -5` Never assume changes to static content are live — Docker rebuild and stack deploy are required for Next.js projects. ", "path": "software-development/ecommerce-codebase-audit/SKILL.md", "skill_dir": "/root/.hermes/skills/software-development/ecommerce-codebase-audit", "linked_files": {"references": ["references/el-viajero-auth-flow.md", "references/visual-asset-audit-workflow.md", "references/nexa-paraguay-content-update.md", "references/el-viajero-admin-panel.md", "references/supabase-debugging-patterns.md"]}, "usage_hint": "To view linked files, call skill_view(name, file_path) where file_path is e.g. 'references/api.md' or 'assets/config.yaml'", "required_environment_variables": [], "required_commands": [], "missing_required_environment_variables": [], "missing_credential_files": [], "missing_required_commands": [], "setup_needed": false, "setup_skipped": false, "readiness_status": "available"}

{"code": "import json
from hermes_tools import terminal

with open("/root/nexa-paraguay/content/en.json") as f:
    d = json.load(f)

print("======= COMPLETE SITE AUDIT - NEXA PARAGUAY =======")
print()

# 1. PAGES INVENTORY
print("=== 1. PAGE INVENTORY ===")
nav_items = d["navigation"]["navItems"]
def flatten(items):
    for item in items:
        if "children" in item:
            for c in item["children"]:
                yield (c["label"], c["href"], True)
        else:
            yield (item["label"], item.get("href",""), False)
nav_list = list(flatten(nav_items))
for label, href, is_child in nav_list:
    print(f"  Nav: {label:30s} -> {href}")
print()

# All page files

content/blog/posts.json:17:      "content": "# Guía Completa: Cómo Obtener la Residencia en Paraguay en 2024\n\nParaguay se ha convertido en uno de los destinos más atractivos para expatriados, emprendedores e inversores de toda Latinoamérica y el mundo. Su crecimiento económico sostenido, régimen fiscal favorable y calidad de vida hacen de este país una opción ideal para establecer una nueva base de operaciones.\n\n## Requisitos Principales\n\nPara obtener la residencia en Paraguay, necesitarás:\n\n1. **Pasaporte vigente** con al menos 6 meses de validez\n2. **Certificado de antecedentes penales** de tu país de origen\n3. **Certificado de nacimiento** apostillado\n4. **Comprobante de solvencia económica**\n5. **Certificado médico** expedido en Paraguay\n\n## El Proceso Paso a Paso\n\n### Paso 1: Preparación Documental\nEl primer paso es reunir todos los documentos necesarios. Es crucial que estos documentos estén correctamente apostillados según el Convenio de La Haya.\n\n### Paso 2: Ingreso a Paraguay\nDeberás ingresar al país como turista y comenzar el proceso de radicación. Durante esta etapa, te recomendamos asesoría legal especializada.\n\n### Paso 3: Presentación de Expediente\nCon todos los documentos en orden, se presenta el expediente ante la Dirección General de Migraciones.\n\n### Paso 4: Aprobación y Cédula\nUna vez aprobada la residencia, podrás tramitar tu cédula de identidad paraguaya.\n\n## Tiempos Estimados\n\n- Preparación documental: 2-4 semanas\n- Tramitación en Paraguay: 4-8 semanas\n- Obtención de cédula: 1-2 semanas\n\n**Tiempo total estimado: 8-12 semanas**\n\n## Beneficios de la Residencia Paraguaya\n\n1. **Acceso al MERCOSUR** y libre circulación en países miembros\n2. **Régimen fiscal favorable** con impuestos competitivos\n3. **Posibilidad de constitución de sociedades** con facilidades únicas\n4. **Apertura de cuentas bancarias** sin complicaciones\n5. **Compra de propiedades** con los mismos derechos que nacionales\n\n## Consejos de Nexa Paraguay\n\nBasándonos en nuestra experiencia con más de 250 clientes:\n\n- **Planifica con anticipación**: El proceso requiere tiempo y preparación\n- **Asesórate con expertos**: Un error documental puede retrasar meses el proceso\n- **Conoce la cultura local**: Paraguay tiene una cultura empresarial única basada en relaciones personales\n- **Evalúa opciones de inversión**: El mercado inmobiliario paraguayo ofrece excelentes oportunidades\n\n## Próximos Pasos\n\n¿Listo para comenzar tu proceso de radicación? Agenda una consulta gratuita con nuestro equipo y descubre qué programa de Nexa Paraguay se adapta mejor a tus necesidades.\n\n---\n\n*Este artículo es informativo. Las regulaciones migratorias pueden cambiar. Consulta siempre con profesionales actualizados.*"
content/blog/posts.json:33:      "content": "# Guía para Extranjeros: Cómo Comprar Propiedades en Paraguay\n\nEl mercado inmobiliario paraguayo se ha posicionado como uno de los más atractivos de Sudamérica para inversores extranjeros. Con precios competitivos, alta rentabilidad y un marco legal favorable, comprar propiedades en Paraguay es más simple de lo que imaginas.\n\n## ¿Pueden los Extranjeros Comprar Propiedad en Paraguay?\n\n**Sí, absolutamente.** La Constitución paraguaya garantiza a extranjeros los mismos derechos de propiedad que a nacionales, con una sola excepción: la propiedad rural en zonas de frontera (50km), que requiere autorización especial.\n\n## Requisitos para Comprar\n\n### Si ya tienes Residencia Paraguaya\n- Cédula de identidad vigente\n- RUC (Registro Único de Contribuyente)\n- Solvencia fiscal\n\n### Si no tienes Residencia\n- Pasaporte vigente\n- Representante legal local (recomendado)\n- Poder notarial (si compras a distancia)\n\n## El Proceso de Compra\n\n### Paso 1: Búsqueda y Due Diligence\n- Identificación de propiedades según objetivo (inversión, vivienda, comercial)\n- Verificación de títulos de propiedad\n- Confirmación de no existencia de gravámenes\n- Revisión de impuestos municipales al día\n\n### Paso 2: Negociación y Reserva\n- Negociación de precio y condiciones\n- Firma de pre-contrato o reserva\n- Depósito de garantía (típicamente 10%)\n\n### Paso 3: Escrituración\n- Firma de escritura pública ante escribano\n- Transferencia de fondos\n- Registro de la propiedad\n\n### Paso 4: Posescrituración\n- Registro en Dirección General de Registros Públicos\n- Cambio de titularidad en servicios\n- Obtención de plano aprobado (si aplica)\n\n## Costos Asociados\n\n| Concepto | Porcentaje | Notas |\n|----------|------------|-------|\n| Impuesto Inmobiliario | 0.5% - 1% anual | Sobre valor fiscal |\n| Impuesto a la Renta (venta) | 10% | Solo sobre ganancia de capital |\n| Impuesto de Sellos | 0.5% - 2% | En escrituración |\n| Honorarios Escribano | 1% - 1.5% | Negociable |\n| Registro de Propiedad | 0.5% - 1% | Sobre valor de escritura |\n| Comisión Corretaje | 3% - 5% | Típicamente paga vendedor |\n\n## Zonas Más Atractivas para Inversión\n\n### Asunción y Gran Asunción\n- **Villa Morra**: Barrio premium, alta valorización\n- **Las Carmelitas**: Seguridad y exclusividad\n- **Mburucuyá**: Zona residencial en crecimiento\n- **San Lorenzo**: Precios accesibles con buena rentabilidad\n\n### Zonas Turísticas\n- **San Bernardino**: Lago Ypacaraí, alta demanda turística\n- **Areguá**: Capital del artesanato, creciente interés\n- **Ciudad del Este**: Centro comercial binacional\n\n### Agro\n- **Alto Paraná**: Tierra productiva para soja\n- **San Pedro**: Precios competitivos, alto rendimiento\n- **Caaguazú**: Creciente demanda agropecuaria\n\n## Rentabilidad Esperada\n\n### Inversión Residencial (Alquiler)\n- **Retorno anual**: 6% - 10% en dólares\n- **Plusvalía**: 8% - 12% anual en zonas premium\n- **Payback**: 10-15 años\n\n### Inversión Comercial\n- **Retorno anual**: 8% - 15%\n- **Más alto riesgo, más alta rentabilidad**\n\n### Inversión Agropecuaria\n- **Retorno anual**: 10% - 20%\n- **Requiere conocimiento técnico**\n\n## Errores Comunes a Evitar\n\n### 1. No Verificar Títulos\nSiempre contrata un abogado para hacer due diligence completa.\n\n### 2. Ignorar Impuestos Municipales\nVerifica que no haya deudas acumuladas que puedan afectar la compra.\n\n### 3. Comprar sin Ver el Inmueble\nSi no puedes viajar, contrata un representante de confianza.\n\n### 4. No Considerar Costos de Mantenimiento\nLos costos de mantenimiento pueden representar 1-3% del valor anual.\n\n## Servicios de Nexa Paraguay\n\nOfrecemos acompañamiento integral en la compra de propiedades:\n\n1. **Búsqueda estratégica** según tu perfil de inversión\n2. **Due diligence legal completa**\n3. **Negociación** con vendedores y corredores\n4. **Acompañamiento en escrituración**\n5. **Gestión post-compra** (servicios, alquileres, etc.)\n\n---\n\n*¿Interesado en invertir en propiedades paraguayas? Agenda una consulta con nuestro equipo inmobiliario.*"
content/blog/posts.json:49:      "content": "# Apertura de Cuenta Bancaria en Paraguay para Extranjeros\n\nEl sistema bancario paraguayo ha evolucionado significativamente en los últimos años, ofreciendo servicios modernos y competitivos para residentes y no residentes. Abrir una cuenta bancaria es un paso esencial para establecerse en el país.\n\n## Tipos de Cuentas Disponibles\n\n### Cuentas en Guaraníes (PYG)\n- Moneda local\n- Menores costos de transacción nacional\n- Ideal para operaciones locales\n\n### Cuentas en Dólares (USD)\n- Protección contra inflación\n- Ideal para ahorro e inversión\n- Recomendada para extranjeros\n\n### Cuentas en Reales (BRL)\n- Útil para comercio con Brasil\n- Menor costo de cambio\n\n## Bancos Principales en Paraguay\n\n### Banco Continental\n- Mayor banco privado del país\n- Excelente plataforma digital\n- Buena atención a empresas\n\n### Banco Itaú Paraguay\n- Parte del grupo brasileño\n- Fuerte en comercio internacional\n- Buena red de sucursales\n\n### Banco Basa\n- Tradicional y conservador\n- Excelente para grandes empresas\n- Servicios de private banking\n\n### Banco Atlas\n- Enfocado en pymes\n- Innovador en productos digitales\n- Buena opción para startups\n\n### Banco Sudameris\n- Creciente presencia internacional\n- Fuerte en comercio exterior\n\n## Requisitos para Abrir Cuenta\n\n### Cuenta Personal (con Residencia)\n1. Cédula de identidad paraguaya\n2. RUC (Registro Único de Contribuyente)\n3. Comprobante de domicilio\n4. Referencias comerciales o laborales\n5. Carta de trabajo o declaración de renta\n\n### Cuenta Empresarial\n1. Escritura de constitución de sociedad\n2. RUC de la empresa\n3. Cédulas de socios/directores\n4. Acta de asamblea autorizando apertura\n5. Contrato social vigente\n6. Estados financieros (empresas existentes)\n\n### Cuenta para No Residentes\n- Pasaporte vigente\n- Referencias bancarias internacionales\n- Comprobante de origen de fondos\n- Entrevista personal obligatoria\n- Depósito inicial más alto\n\n## Proceso de Apertura\n\n### Paso 1: Elegir el Banco\nConsidera:\n- Tarifas de mantenimiento\n- Costos de transferencias internacionales\n- Calidad de banca digital\n- Proximidad de sucursales\n\n### Paso 2: Reunir Documentación\nRevisa los requisitos específicos del banco elegido.\n\n### Paso 3: Entrevista Bancaria\n- Evaluación de perfil de riesgo\n- Conocimiento del cliente (KYC)\n- Verificación de documentos\n\n### Paso 4: Firma de Contratos\n- Contrato de cuenta\n- Reglamento de operaciones\n- Términos de servicios digitales\n\n### Paso 5: Depósito Inicial y Activación\n- Depósito inicial (varía según tipo de cuenta)\n- Recibo de tarjeta de débito\n- Activación de banca en línea\n\n## Tiempos de Apertura\n\n| Tipo de Cuenta | Tiempo Estimado |\n|----------------|------------------|\n| Personal (residente) | 24-72 horas |\n| Empresarial | 3-7 días hábiles |\n| No residente | 1-2 semanas |\n| Cuenta VIP/Empresarial | 1-2 días |\n\n## Costos Típicos\n\n| Concepto | Costo Aproximado |\n|----------|-------------------|\n| Apertura de cuenta | Gratis - USD 50 |\n| Mantenimiento mensual | USD 3 - 15 |\n| Transferencias nacionales | USD 1 - 5 |\n| Transferencias internacionales | USD 25 - 75 |\n| Tarjeta de débito | USD 5 - 15/año |\n| Tarjeta de crédito | USD 20 - 100/año |\n\n## Consideraciones para Extranjeros\n\n### Ventajas\n- Banca estable y regulada\n- Depósitos asegurados (hasta cierto monto)\n- Posibilidad de cuentas en dólares\n- Transferencias SWIFT disponibles\n\n### Desafíos\n- Proceso más complejo sin residencia\n- Requiere presencia personal\n- Límites en montos según perfil\n\n## Recomendación Nexa Paraguay\n\nPara una apertura exitosa:\n\n1. **Obtén tu residencia primero**: Facilita enormemente el proceso\n2. **Prepara documentación completa**: Evita retrasos\n3. **Evalúa múltiples bancos**: Compara servicios y costos\n4. **Considera cuentas en dólares**: Mejor para extranjeros\n5. **Activa todos los servicios digitales**: Banca online, app móvil\n\n## Nuestro Servicio de Acompañamiento\n\nIncluimos en nuestros programas:\n- Preparación de documentación\n- Agendamiento de entrevistas bancarias\n- Acompañamiento en firma de documentos\n- Coordinación con oficiales de cuenta\n- Activación de servicios digitales\n\n---\n\n*¿Necesitas ayuda con la apertura de tu cuenta bancaria? Es parte de nuestro servicio integral.*"
content/blog/posts.json:65:      "content": "# Emprender en Paraguay: Oportunidades de Negocio 2024\n\nParaguay se ha convertido en un destino emergente para emprendedores de toda Latinoamérica. Con un ambiente de negocios favorable, costos competitivos y una economía en crecimiento, las oportunidades son abundantes para quienes saben dónde buscar.\n\n## Por Qué Emprender en Paraguay\n\n### Ventajas del Ecosistema Paraguayo\n\n1. **Régimen Fiscal Competitivo**\n   - 10% de impuesto a la renta empresarial\n   - 10% de IVA\n   - Exenciones para zonas francas\n\n2. **Costos Operativos Bajos**\n   - 40-60% más barato que Buenos Aires o Montevideo\n   - Mano de obra calificada a costos competitivos\n   - Alquileres accesibles\n\n3. **Ubicación Estratégica**\n   - Centro de Sudamérica\n   - Acceso a MERCOSUR (mercado de 300M personas)\n   - Cercanía a Brasil y Argentina\n\n4. **Estabilidad Macroeconómica**\n   - Crecimiento sostenido del PBI\n   - Inflación controlada\n   - Sistema cambiario estable\n\n## Sectores Más Prometedores\n\n### 1. Tecnología y Software\n**Oportunidad: ★★★★★**\n\nParaguay está viviendo un boom tecnológico:\n- Desarrollo de software para exportación\n- Fintech y servicios financieros digitales\n- E-commerce y logística\n- Agrotech\n\n**Caso de Éxito**: Una startup de software paraguaya fue adquirida por USD 10M en 2023.\n\n### 2. Agroindustria y Agrotech\n**Oportunidad: ★★★★★**\n\nParaguay es potencia agrícola:\n- 4to exportador mundial de soja\n- Producción de carne de calidad\n- Tecnificación del campo\n- Exportación de alimentos procesados\n\n**Oportunidades específicas**:\n- Sistemas de riego inteligente\n- Logística agrícola\n- Procesamiento de granos\n- Agricultura orgánica\n\n### 3. Energías Renovables\n**Oportunidad: ★★★★☆**\n\n100% de energía eléctrica es renovable (Itaipú + Yacyretá):\n- Venta de energía limpia\n- Instalación de paneles solares\n- Consultoría energética\n- Minería de Bitcoin (energía barata)\n\n### 4. Turismo y Hospitalidad\n**Oportunidad: ★★★★☆**\n\nTurismo en crecimiento acelerado:\n- Ecoturismo\n- Turismo de eventos\n- Hoteles boutique\n- Experiencias locales únicas\n\nDestinos emergentes:\n- Saltos del Monday\n- Lago Ypacaraí\n- Misiones Jesuíticas\n- Chaco paraguayo\n\n### 5. Sector Inmobiliario\n**Oportunidad: ★★★★☆**\n\nMercado inmobiliario dinámico:\n- Construcción residencial\n- Desarrollo de oficinas\n- Propiedades industriales\n- Coworking spaces\n\nRentabilidades atractivas:\n- 6-10% anual en alquileres\n- Plusvalía de 8-12% anual\n\n### 6. Servicios Profesionales B2B\n**Oportunidad: ★★★★☆**\n\nDemanda creciente de:\n- Consultoría de negocios\n- Marketing digital\n- Servicios legales especializados\n- Contabilidad internacional\n- Recursos humanos\n\n### 7. Salud y Bienestar\n**Oportunidad: ★★★☆☆**\n\nSector con alto potencial:\n- Medicina prepaga\n- Centros de bienestar\n- Nutrición deportiva\n- Turismo médico\n\n### 8. Educación y Capacitación\n**Oportunidad: ★★★☆☆**\n\nDemanda constante:\n- Institutos de inglés\n- Cursos técnicos\n- Capacitación corporativa\n- Edtech\n\n## Inversión Requerida por Sector\n\n| Sector | Inversión Mínima | ROI Esperado |\n|--------|-------------------|----------------|\n| Tecnología | USD 5,000 - 20,000 | 30-50% |\n| Agroindustria | USD 50,[REDACTED_PHONE],000 | 15-25% |\n| Turismo | USD 30,[REDACTED_PHONE],000 | 20-30% |\n| Inmobiliario | USD 100,000+ | 12-20% |\n| Servicios B2B | USD 2,000 - 10,000 | 40-60% |\n\n## Pasos para Emprender en Paraguay\n\n### 1. Investigación de Mercado\n- Análisis de competencia\n- Estudio de demanda\n- Identificación de nicho\n\n### 2. Constitución Legal\n- Elección de tipo de sociedad\n- Trámites de registro\n- Obtención de RUC\n\n### 3. Apertura de Cuenta Bancaria\n- Selección de banco\n- Gestión de documentación\n- Activación de servicios\n\n### 4. Cumplimiento Fiscal\n- Inscripción en SET\n- Configuración de facturación\n- Planificación fiscal\n\n### 5. Operaciones\n- Contratación de personal\n- Locación de oficinas/instalaciones\n- Marketing y lanzamiento\n\n## Apoyo Gubernamental\n\n### Incentivos Disponibles\n- **Zona Franca Paraguay**: Exención fiscal total\n- **Maquila**: Beneficios para exportadores\n- **Sistema de drawback**: Devolución de impuestos\n- **Inversiones en turismo**: Beneficios específicos\n\n### Instituciones de Apoyo\n- **REDIEX**: Promoción de exportaciones\n- **MIC**: Ministerio de Industria y Comercio\n- **Banco Central**: Regulación y estabilidad\n- **Cámara de Comercio**: Networking y apoyo\n\n## Casos de Éxito Recientes\n\n### Startup Tech (SaaS)\nInversión inicial: USD 15,000\nFacturación año 3: USD 500,000\nEmpleados: 25 personas\n\n### Exportadora Agropecuaria\nInversión inicial: USD 80,000\nExportación anual: USD 2M\nMercados: 8 países\n\n### Hotel Boutique\nInversión inicial: USD 120,000\nOcupación: 75% promedio anual\nROI: 22% anual\n\n## Recomendaciones de Nexa Paraguay\n\n1. **Comienza con residencia**: Facilita todos los trámites empresariales\n2. **Asesórate desde el inicio**: Un buen contador y abogado valen su peso en oro\n3. **Entiende la cultura**: Las relaciones personales son fundamentales\n4. **Sé paciente**: Los trámites pueden ser lentos, pero son seguros\n5. **Piensa en grande**: Paraguay es una plataforma para Sudamérica\n\n## Nuestros Servicios para Emprendedores\n\n- Asesoría en elección de sector\n- Constitución de sociedades\n- Planificación fiscal\n- Apertura de cuentas bancarias\n- Permisos y licencias\n- Contratación de personal\n- Networking y contactos\n\n---\n\n*¿Tienes una idea de negocio? Agenda una consulta estratégica gratuita.*"

{"content": "# Nexa Paraguay — Complete Site Audit (May 5, 2026)

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

**Status:** Content restructured to match client questionnaire. Core pages (Home, Programs, Process, About) updated in all 4 locales. Landing pages updated. Critical gaps remain in analytics, SEO descriptions, navigation, and localizations flagged for professional translation.

---

## 1. PAGE INVENTORY

### Navigation (11 items linked)

| Home | `/` | ✅ |
| Programs | `/programas` | ✅ |
| Why Paraguay | `/por-que-paraguay` | ✅ |
| Process | `/proceso` | ✅ |
| About | `/sobre` | ✅ |
| FAQ | `/faq` | ✅ |
| Benelux | `/benelux` | ✅ |
| Blog | `/blog` | ✅ |
| Resources | `/recursos` | ✅ |
| Press | `/prensa` | ✅ |
| Contact | `/contacto` | ✅ |

### Orphan Pages (10 — NOT linked from navigation)

|------|-------------|-------|
| `asistente` | `intakeWizardPage` | ✅ Now has content. Missing from nav — should be linked or removed |
| `calidad-de-vida` | `qualityOfLifePage` | 🟡 Exists but not nav-linked. Footer links to it |
| `casos-de-exito` | `caseStudiesPage` | ❌ Has generic content. Not nav-linked |
| `comparacion` | `comparisonPage` | 🟡 Has content. Not nav-linked |
| `empresa` | `landingEmpresa` | ✅ Now updated. LinkedIn ad landing page |
| `fundador` | `founderPage` | ❌ Not nav-linked. Missing founder content matching new story? |
| `glosario` | `glossaryPage` | 🟡 Has content, footer-linked |
| `inversor` | `landingInversor` | ✅ LinkedIn ad landing page |
| `lifestyle` | `landingLifestyle` | ✅ LinkedIn ad landing page |
| `privacidad` | `privacyPage` | ✅ Has content. Not nav-linked (only footer) |
| `trust` | `landingTrust` | ✅ LinkedIn ad landing page |

**Action:** `asistente`, `calidad-de-vida`, `privacidad` should be in nav or footer or deleted. `fundador` needs founder content or should be removed.

---

## 2. SEO

### Page Titles & Descriptions

| Page | Title | Description | Status |
|------|-------|-------------|--------|
| Home | ✅ Updated (client-aligned) | Old: "Residency, company and bank account…" | 🟡 Description still references old "one program, one trip" narrative |
| Programs | ✅ OK | OK | ✅ |
| Process | ✅ Updated | ✅ Updated to match new steps | ✅ |
| About | ✅ OK | ✅ Updated with founder story | ✅ |
| FAQ | ✅ OK | OK | ✅ |
| Contact | ✅ OK | "Book a free 30-minute consultation" is fine | ✅ |

### Schema.org
- `schemaType: "LocalBusiness"` on homepage — ✅ set
- `schemaType: "Service"` on programs page — ✅ set
- No FAQ schema, no Article schema for blog posts — 🟡 missing
- **No local-business structured data** (address, phone, hours) in JSON-LD — ❌ missing

**Action:** Add FAQ schema, Article schema for blog, LocalBusiness JSON-LD with address/phone/hours.

---

## 3. CONTENT COMPLETENESS

### Locale Comparison
| Locale | Lines | Missing Keys vs EN |
|--------|-------|---------------------|
| en | 2405 | — (reference) |
| es | 2420 | None |
| nl | 2414 | `seo` (top-level `seo` key) — needs to be added |
| de | 2417 | `seo` (top-level `seo` key), notes says "Seeded MT output. Needs professional translation" |

**🟡 German has a warning: "Seeded MT output. Needs professional DE translation before production."** Flagged in LAUNCH.md item #8.

**🟡 nl.json and de.json missing the top-level `seo` object.** This contains `siteDescription`, `siteKeywords`, `ogImage`, etc. for meta tags.

### Content Quality
- Services: ✅ 3 groups, 9 items in all locales
- Story: ✅ 3 paragraphs in all locales
- Process: ✅ 5 steps in all locales
- Hero images: ✅ Localized per locale (en/es/nl/de)

---

## 4. SERVICES SECTION

**Status: ✅ Implemented from client responses**
- Homepage: ✅ Via `home.services` with variant `grouped-cards`
- Programs page: ✅ Also references `home.services`
- Dedicated services page: ❌ **Does not exist.** No `/servicios` page. Services only appear as a section embedded in Home and Programs pages.

---

## 5. STORY SECTION

**Status: ✅ Implemented from client responses**
- About page hero: Updated to "Experience Turned into Expertise"
- About page story section: Added with 3 paragraphs of founder narrative
- All 4 locales: ✅

---

## 6. PROCESS STEPS

**Status: ✅ Implemented from client responses**
1. Select Your Services ✅
2. Parallel Processing ✅
3. The Operational Day ✅
4. Cédula & Financial Activation ✅
5. Settlement & Lifestyle ✅

All 4 locales ✅. Home page + Process page + 4 landing pages all updated ✅.

---

All now consistent with the main process narrative.

---

## 8. PRICING

4 tiers — unchanged from original. Client questionnaire didn't mention pricing changes:

|---------|-------|-------------|
| Paraguay Base | USD 2,900 | Residency + ID |
| Paraguay Business | USD 4,400+ | Residency + company + bank |
| Paraguay Investor | USD 6,900+ | Business + 12mo support |
| Land Purchase | Quote on request | Full advisory |

**Note:** Client described 9 a-la-carte services, but site still sells 4 bundles. The services section shows a-la-carte, packages show bundles. This is the intended hierarchy but should be confirmed with client.

{"code": "import json

# Read current site.json and content
with open("/root/nexa-paraguay/site.json") as f:
    site = json.load(f)

# 1. INTEGRATIONS - mark placeholders as needing real values (can't guess real IDs)
# We flag them but don't replace with fake values
with open("/root/nexa-paraguay/content/en.json") as f:
    en = json.load(f)

print("=== 1. INTEGRATIONS (site.json) ===")
print(f"GA4: {site['integrations']['analytics']['ga4']['measurementId']}")
print(f"HubSpot: {site['integrations']['hubspot']['portalId']}")
print(f"Mailchimp: {site['integrations']['mailchimp']['audienceId']}")
print("These need real values from the client - can't guess.")
print()

# 2. LANDING PAGE CTAs - fix the 2 per locale pointing to #
print("=== 2. LANDING PAGE CTAs ===")
calendly_base = "https://calendly.com/nexaparaguay/consulta"

# Map landing pages to their UTM campaigns
utm_map = {
    "landingInversor": "utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=paid&utm_campaign=investor",
    "landingTrust": "utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=paid&utm_campaign=trust",
    "landingLifestyle": "utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=paid&utm_campaign=lifestyle",
    "landingEmpresa": "utm_source=linkedin&utm_medium=paid&utm_campaign=company"
}

lang_suffixes = {
    "en": "en",
    "es": "es",
    "nl": "nl",
    "de": "de"
}

for lang in ["en", "es", "nl", "de"]:
    path = f"/root/nexa-paraguay/content/{lang}.json"
    with open(path) as f:
        d = json.load(f)
    
    suffix = lang_suffixes[lang]
    fixes = 0
    
    for lp_key, utm in utm_map.items():
        if lp_key in d:
            lp = d[lp_key]
            
            # Fix CTA in hero
            cta_key = "ctaPrimaryHref" if "ctaPrimaryHref" in lp.get("hero", {}) else None
            if cta_key and lp["hero"].get(cta_key, "") == "#":
                lp["hero"][cta_key] = f"{calendly_base}?{utm}&utm_content=cta-hero-primary-{suffix}"
                fixes += 1
            
            # Fix CTA in process section
            if "process" in lp and "ctaHref" in lp["process"]:
                if lp["process"]["ctaHref"] == "#":
                    lp["process"]["ctaHref"] = f"{calendly_base}?{utm}&utm_content=cta-process-{suffix}"
                    fixes += 1
            
            # Fix CTA in programs section
            if "programs" in lp:
                for tier in lp["programs"].get("tiers", []):
                    if tier.get("ctaLabel") and tier.get("ctaHref", "") == "#":
                        tier["ctaHref"] = f"{calendly_base}?{utm}&utm_content=cta-{lp_key}-{suffix}"
                        fixes += 1
            
            # Fix final CTA
            if "cta" in lp:
                if lp["cta"].get("buttonHref", "") == "#":
                    lp["cta"]["buttonHref"] = f"{calendly_base}?{utm}&utm_content=cta-final-{suffix}"
                    fixes += 1
    
    # Also standardize any non-UTM Calendly links to include UTM
    for lp_key, utm in utm_map.items():
        if lp_key in d:
            lp = d[lp_key]
            suffix = lang_suffixes[lang]
            
            # Hero primary
            if "hero" in lp and "ctaPrimaryHref" in lp["hero"]:
                href = lp["hero"]["ctaPrimaryHref"]
                if "calendly.com" in href and "utm_source" not in href:
                    lp["hero"]["ctaPrimaryHref"] = f"{calendly_base}?{utm}&utm_content=cta-hero-primary-{suffix}"
                    fixes += 1
            
            # Process CTA
            if "process" in lp and "ctaHref" in lp["process"]:
                href = lp["process"]["ctaHref"]
                if "calendly.com" in href and "utm_source" not in href:
                    lp["process"]["ctaHref"] = f"{calendly_base}?{utm}&utm_content=cta-process-{suffix}"
                    fixes += 1
            
            # Final CTA
            if "cta" in lp:
                href = lp["cta"].get("buttonHref", "")
                if "calendly.com" in href and "utm_source" not in href:
                    lp["cta"]["buttonHref"] = f"{calendly_base}?{utm}&utm_content=cta-final-{suffix}"
                    fixes += 1
            
            # Program tier CTAs
            if "programs" in lp:
                for tier in lp["programs"].get("tiers", []):
                    href = tier.get("ctaHref", "")
                    if "calendly.com" in href and "utm_source" not in href:
                        tier["ctaHref"] = f"{calendly_base}?{utm}&utm_content=cta-{lp_key}-{suffix}"
                        fixes += 1
    
    with open(path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(d, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
    
    print(f"{lang}: {fixes} CTA fixes")

print()

# 3. SEO - update homepage description
print("=== 3. HOME SEO DESCRIPTION ===")
for lang in ["en", "es", "nl", "de"]:
    path = f"/root/nexa-paraguay/content/{lang}.json"
    with open(path) as f:
        d = json.load(f)
    
    old_desc = d["home"]["seo"]["description"]
    new_descs = {
        "en": "Residency, banking, real estate and settlement — all coordinated by Nexa Paraguay. Services you choose, a streamlined process, and a professional team by your side.",
        "es": "Residencia, banca, bienes raíces e instalación — todo coordinado por Nexa Paraguay. Servicios a tu elección, proceso optimizado y equipo profesional.",
        "nl": "Verblijf, bankieren, vastgoed en vestiging — alles gecoördineerd door Nexa Paraguay. Diensten naar keuze, gestroomlijnd proces en een professioneel team.",
        "de": "Aufenthalt, Bankwesen, Immobilien und Niederlassung — alles koordiniert von Nexa Paraguay. Dienstleistungen nach Wahl, optimierter Prozess und ein professionelles Team."
    }
    d["home"]["seo"]["description"] = new_descs[lang]
    print(f"{lang}: '{old_desc[:50]}...' -> '{new_descs[lang][:50]}...'")
    
    with open(path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(d, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)

print()

# 4. Fix nl.json and de.json missing top-level seo key
print("=== 4. MISSING SEO KEYS ===")
for lang in ["nl", "de"]:
    path = f"/root/nexa-paraguay/content/{lang}.json"
    with open(path) as f:
        d = json.load(f)
    
    if "seo" not in d:
        seo_content = {
            "siteDescription": f"Nexa Paraguay — {'Jouw verhuizing naar Paraguay, volledig begeleid' if lang == 'nl' else 'Ihr Umzug nach Paraguay, komplett betreut'}",
            "siteKeywords": ["Nexa Paraguay", "residency Paraguay", "relocation Paraguay", "company Paraguay", "bank account Paraguay"],
            "ogImage": "/sites/nexa-paraguay/images/brand/og-default.webp",
            "twitterHandle": "@nexaparaguay"
        }
        d["seo"] = seo_content
        with open(path, "w") as f:
            json.dump(d, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
        print(f"{lang}: added top-level seo key")
    else:
        print(f"{lang}: seo key already exists")

print()

# 5. Stats - add a note flagging them as unconfirmed
print("=== 5. STATS NOTE ===")
for lang in ["en", "es", "nl", "de"]:
    path = f"/root/nexa-paraguay/content/{lang}.json"
    with open(path) as f:
        d = json.load(f)
    
    d["home"]["stats"]["_note"] = "Stats values not confirmed by client questionnaire (May 5 2026). Verify before production."
    
    with open(path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(d, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
    print(f"{lang}: added stats note")

print()

# 6. UTM standardization already done in step 2
print("=== 6. UTM params standardized ===")
print("Done as part of step 2")

print()

# 7. Create dedicated services page
print("=== 7. SERVICES PAGE ===")
svc_page = {
    "slug": "servicios",
    "titleKey": "servicesPage.seo.title",
    "descriptionKey": "servicesPage.seo.description",
    "sections": [
        {"id": "header", "variant": "standard", "content": "navigation"},
        {"id": "hero", "variant": "minimal", "content": "servicesPage.hero"},
        {"id": "services", "variant": "grouped-cards", "content": "servicesPage.detail", "styling": {"padding": "section"}},
        {"id": "cta-banner", "variant": "gradient", "content": "servicesPage.cta"},
        {"id": "footer", "variant": "standard", "content": "footer"},
        {"id": "whatsapp-float", "variant": "standard", "content": "whatsapp"}
    ]
}

with open("/root/nexa-paraguay/pages/servicios.json", "w") as f:
    json.dump(svc_page, f, indent=2)
print("pages/servicios.json created")

# Add servicesPage content to all 4 locales
for lang in ["en", "es", "nl", "de"]:
    path = f"/root/nexa-paraguay/content/{lang}.json"
    with open(path) as f:

        d = json.load(f)

        "es": "Nuestra Fundadora — Nexa Paraguay",
        "nl": "Onze Oprichter — Nexa Paraguay",
        "de": "Unsere Gründerin — Nexa Paraguay"

    descs = {
        "en": "Meet the founder of Nexa Paraguay: firsthand experience in international relocation, married to a Dutch national.",
        "es": "Conoce a la fundadora de Nexa Paraguay: experiencia de primera mano en mudanza internacional, casada con un neerlandés.",
        "nl": "Ontmoet de oprichter van Nexa Paraguay: ervaring uit eerste hand met internationale verhuizing, getrouwd met een Nederlander.",

        "es": "Experiencia Convertida en Conocimiento",
        "nl": "Ervaring omgezet in Expertise",

    hero_subs = {

        "es": "La historia detrás de Nexa Paraguay — de la experiencia personal a la misión profesional.",
        "nl": "Het verhaal achter Nexa Paraguay — van persoonlijke ervaring naar professionele missie.",
        "de": "Die Geschichte hinter Nexa Paraguay — von persönlicher Erfahrung zu professioneller Mission."
    }

        d["founderPage"]["seo"]["description"] = descs[lang]
        d["founderPage"]["hero"]["headline"] = hero_headlines[lang]
        d["founderPage"]["hero"]["subheadline"] = hero_subs[lang]
        
        # Add story body
        d["founderPage"]["story"] = {
            "title": {"en":"Our Story","es":"Nuestra Historia","nl":"Ons Verhaal","de":"Unsere Geschichte"}[lang],
            "paragraphs": d["aboutPage"]["story"]["paragraphs"]
        }
    else:
        d["founderPage"] = {
            "seo": {"title": titles[lang], "description": descs[lang]},
            "hero": {"headline": hero_headlines[lang], "subheadline": hero_subs[lang]},
            "story": {
                "title": {"en":"Our Story","es":"Nuestra Historia","nl":"Ons Verhaal","de":"Unsere Geschichte"}[lang],
                "paragraphs": d["aboutPage"]["story"]["paragraphs"]
            }
        }
    
    with open(path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(d, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)

for lang in ["en", "es", "nl", "de"]:

print()

print("ALL DONE")

print()

print("=== DUE DILIGENCE COVERAGE ===")
for g in d["home"]["services"]["groups"]:

        for item in g["items"]:
            if "due" in item["title"].lower():
                print(f"Due diligence desc: '{item['description']}'")
                has_rent = "rent" in item["description"].lower()
                has_buy = "buy" in item["description"].lower()
                print(f"Covers rent: {has_rent}, Covers buy: {has_buy}")

print()

# 3. APOSTILLE - client lists as separate service #3
print("=== APOSTILLE ===")
for g in d["home"]["services"]["groups"]:
    if g["id"] == "residency-legal":
        for item in g["items"]:
            if "apostille" in item["title"].lower():
                print(f"Apostille desc: '{item['description']}'")
                print(f"Covers certified translation: {'certified translation' in item['description'].lower()}")
                print(f"Legal verification: {'legal verification' in item['description'].lower()}")
                print(f"All foreign docs: {'all necessary foreign documents' in item['description'].lower()}")

print()

# 4. CLIENT'S EXACT PHRASING CHECK - word-for-word
client_phrases = {
    "services_intro": "Our Services\nThe section is now clean and focused, appearing as a professional list of solutions.",
    "residency_headline": "1. Residency & Legal Documents\nThe essential first steps for your life in Paraguay.",
    "residency_permanent": "Permanent Residency: Professional management of your residency permit application.",
    "residency_cedula": "Paraguayan ID (Cédula): Full assistance in obtaining your local identification card.",
    "residency_apostille": "Apostille & Translation: We handle the certified translation and legal verification of all necessary foreign documents.",
    "banking_headline": "2. Banking & Tax Setup\nEstablishing your financial footprint in the country.",
    "banking_open": "Bank Account Opening: Guidance and support to successfully open local bank accounts.",
    "banking_ruc": "Tax Registration (RUC): Setup of your Paraguayan taxpayer ID for personal or business use.",
    "realestate_headline": "3. Residential Real Estate\nFinding the right place to call home, whether you are staying for a month or a lifetime.",
    "realestate_rentals": "Apartment & House Rentals: Help navigating the local market to find high-quality rentals.",
    "realestate_purchase": "Property Purchases: Expert assistance in finding and buying residential property.",
    "realestate_due": "Due Diligence: We verify titles and legal standing for any property you intend to rent or buy to ensure your investment is safe.",
    "process_headline": "The Ideal Path to Residency\nA streamlined collaboration between your home country and your new home in Paraguay.",
    "process_step1": "1. Select Your Services\nChoose the specific support you need. We create a personalized checklist of documents you need to gather in your home country, ensuring you don't waste time on unnecessary paperwork.",
    "process_step2": "2. Parallel Processing\nWhile you arrange your documents at home, we begin the work on the ground. We prepare all local filings and arrange for any required Paraguayan translations or legalizations so everything is ready for your arrival.",
    "process_step3": "3. The Operational Day\nWith your documents in hand, you fly to Paraguay. We escort you through all in-person appointments—migration, police records, and identification—consolidating the entire legal process into one efficient day.",
    "process_step4ish": "Once your Paraguayan ID (Cédula) is issued, we activate your financial infrastructure.",
    "process_step5ish": "This includes finding the perfect house or apartment to rent or buy and sourcing a reliable vehicle.",
    "story_mission": "Nexa Paraguay was founded with a single mission: to transform the relocation process from a bureaucratic hurdle into a welcoming experience.",
    "story_dutch": "Our founder is married to a Dutch national and personally navigated the complexities of international relocation and documentation.",
    "story_bestlife": "We believe your move should be more than just \"getting a permit\"—it should be the beginning of your best life here."
}

en_content = json.dumps(d).lower()

print("=== WORD-FOR-WORD PHRASING CHECK ===")
unmatched = []
for key, phrase in client_phrases.items():
    # Check the core semantic words, not exact casing/punctuation
    core_words = phrase.lower().split()
    # Skip short words, check for key nouns
    key_terms = [w.strip(".,:;!?\"'-") for w in core_words if len(w.strip(".,:;!?\"'-")) > 4]
    present = all(term in en_content for term in key_terms)
    if present:
        print(f"  ✓ {key}")
    else:
        unmatched_terms = [t for t in key_terms if t not in en_content]
        print(f"  ✗ {key}: missing terms: {unmatched_terms[:5]}")
        unmatched.append(key)

if unmatched:
    print(f"\n{len(unmatched)} areas need attention")
else:
    print("\nAll client phrasing fully implemented ✓")
"}

with open("/root/nexa-paraguay/content/en.json") as f:
    d = json.load(f)

# 1. TAGLINE doesn't match the About page voice
print("=== 1. VOICE CONSISTENCY ===")
